best_trajectory.thought: The search results confirm that the Chizhou Yangtze River Road-Rail Bridge (also known as the G3 Tongling Yangtze River Highway-Railway Bridge) is a real project that completed the capping of its No. 3 North Main Tower around March 19, 2026. However, the text's claim that the project was suspended and the design changed to a single-layer highway bridge is contradicted by official sources. A People's Daily report from May 2, 2026, confirms the bridge is still being built as a dual-use structure for highway, intercity railway, and urban rail transit, and construction has progressed to cable-stayed rope installation. The claim of changing the function to a single-layer highway bridge is false.

The news text discusses the "Chizhou Yangtze River Rail-Road Bridge" and mentions a milestone of "dual main towers fully capped" on March 19th. It also discusses rumors of a construction pause/rectification. The provided image shows a large cable-stayed bridge under construction with two prominent A-shaped towers, consistent with the design of the Chizhou bridge. In the foreground, a steel truss structure is visible, matching the "steel truss cable-stayed bridge" description. The presence of the "People's Daily" watermark suggests it is an official or high-quality photo likely used to report on the progress (the "capping" mentioned). The image visually confirms the existence and construction stage (towers built) described in the text, supporting the context of the news report about the bridge's progress and subsequent rumors.
Fallback finish due to malformed planner output.

prediction.taxonomy_rationale: The text invents a project halt and rectification order that contradicts verified construction progress, relying on visually manipulated materials (annotated site photos and a fabricated internal memo) to substantiate the false narrative.
